title: Debtor's Rights in Enforcement Proceedings
description: Learn your rights as a debtor. Which items are exempt from enforcement and what protection you can count on during bailiff proceedings.
content:
Debtors Have Rights — Not Just Obligations

Enforcement proceedings serve to satisfy legitimate creditor claims; however, Polish law provides debtors with a number of important protections. The court bailiff is required to act in accordance with the law, and any violations may be challenged by the debtor.

Items Exempt from Enforcement

The Code of Civil Procedure (Art. 829 and following) sets out categories of items and rights that cannot be seized by the bailiff, including:

  • Household furnishings necessary for the debtor and their family for daily living (bed, bedding, underwear, basic household appliances).
  • Work tools necessary for the practice of a profession for a period of one month.
  • Food and fuel supplies for a period of one month.
  • Personal documents, orders, decorations, and items used for religious practice.
  • Medicines and medical equipment necessary due to the health condition of the debtor or family members.
  • Pets — although animals serving productive purposes may be subject to seizure.

Protection of Employment Income

The bailiff cannot seize the debtor's entire salary. The exempt amount equals the minimum wage (gross). In cases of multiple seizures, the total amount seized cannot exceed 3/5 of the salary (for maintenance obligations — up to 3/5 without monetary limits).

Protection of Bank Accounts

In a bank account, the amount equal to 75% of the minimum wage is exempt from seizure — for each month in which salary was credited. The debtor may request the bank to release this amount.

Complaint Against the Bailiff's Actions

If the debtor believes the bailiff violated legal provisions when carrying out enforcement activities, they may file a complaint against the bailiff's actions with the district court having jurisdiction over the office's location. The complaint must be filed within one week of the activity being performed if the debtor was present, or from the date of notification of the activity.

Application to Suspend or Discontinue Proceedings

In certain circumstances, the debtor may apply for suspension of enforcement — for example, when the debt is fully repaid, when the claim is statute-barred, or when a difficult personal situation justifies instalment payments. Discontinuation of proceedings by operation of law occurs when no enforcement activity has been taken for 6 months due to reasons attributable to the creditor.

Settlement with the Creditor

It is worth knowing that a settlement with the creditor is possible at any stage of the proceedings. The bailiff's office is not a party to such an agreement; however, the creditor may withdraw the enforcement application after payment is made — whereupon the proceedings are discontinued.

Have questions about your proceedings? Bailiff's Office No. IX in Otwock provides information on the status of cases and clarifies any procedural doubts. We invite you to contact us.
author: Marcin Starzec
metaTitle: Debtor's Rights in Enforcement Proceedings | Bailiff Otwock
metaDescription: What rights does a debtor have during bailiff enforcement? What is exempt from seizure? How to file a complaint against a bailiff? Practical guide from Otwock.
